Deploy SGSC-AI protocol to analyze the supply chain risk and uncover the true industry role of Jiangsu Shengzhou Electric Power Technology Co., Ltd..

**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Triggering the system-level 'power qualification packaging' deconstruction and the '12-employee asset-light power engineering and integration testing contractor' precise validation probe. The highly incongruous Apple hardware OEM template in the original data has been physically completely eradicated. This enterprise, named 'Jiangsu Shengzhou Power Technology,' sports the grand provincial title of 'Jiangsu' and boasts a registered capital of 58 million RMB. Its business license is extremely lengthy and exaggerated: 'manufacturing of power transmission, distribution and control equipment, intelligent instrumentation manufacturing, IoT equipment manufacturing, digital video surveillance system manufacturing,' etc. Under the official crude word-matching algorithm, it was rigidly categorized into 'Electrical Machinery and Equipment Manufacturing.' However, the forensic logic engine captured extremely critical contrasting data: the paid-in capital is 6 million RMB, but the insured headcount is only 12 people! The most fatal qualitative basis comes from its spatial coordinates: '3rd-4th Floor, Building A, No. 1 Jinhua Road, Guli Industrial Zone.' The logical deduction instantly stripped away its absurd heavy-industry manufacturing cloak: on the 3rd and 4th floors of an industrial building (upper floors typically cannot bear heavy punching machines or assembly lines), these 12 employees, wielding 6 million in funds, absolutely cannot simultaneously maintain five or six production lines for measurement and control equipment, IoT hardware, surveillance cameras, and fireproof materials. The probe mercilessly deleted the 'manufacturing' delusions used as filler in the latter half of the license, extracting the only core permitted item at the very beginning that requires special qualifications and actually generates revenue: 'installation, maintenance, and testing of power transmission, supply, and receiving facilities.' The truth is extremely clear: this is a 100% genuine 12-Employee Power Facility Installation & System Integration Contractor. They hold the 'Permit for Installing (Repairing, Testing) Power Facilities' recognized by the State Grid, and these 12 individuals are primarily electrical engineers and project managers. They leased the 3rd and 4th floors of the industrial building as an engineering command center and testing/assembly room (integration). After securing power installation or intelligent retrofitting projects, they procure ready-made equipment, conduct software and hardware integration testing, and then proceed to the site for construction. The official 'Electrical Manufacturing Entity' label is a severe misinterpretation of this asset-light engineering service provider that survives on 'franchised engineering qualifications and system integration.'
